title: abutilon minarum
intro: Abutilon minarum, also known as the hairy gourd or Indian mallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Abutilon minarum include its ability to reduce pain and swelling, improve digestion, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to create poultices and topical creams for skin conditions and wounds.
